The '''''Kandosii''-type dreadnaught''', named after the [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]] word for ''"indomitable"'', was the largest [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]<ref name="KOTORCG" /> used by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]].<ref name="Kotor15" />

==Characteristics==
===Dimensions===
[[File:MandDSC.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Scan of a ''Kandosii''-type Mandalorian Dreadnaught.]]
The ''Kandosii'' design had sharp, thin lines and were based on a variety of stolen technologies. A notable flaw in their design was that their [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] navigation systems could be crippled by a strategically-placed charge in the rear.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

===Offensive and defensive systems===
''Kandosii''-type dreadnaughts were heavily armed for combat, with five heavy double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries providing the main offensive firepower. They also carried two [[Point-defense laser cannon/Legends|point-defense]] triple [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] batteries placed at certain places as to give full hull coverage. Each dreadnaught also had two super-heavy [[Concussion missile launcher/Legends|concussion missile batteries]] that could bombard capital ships,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> or hold nuclear missiles to bombard planets, as seen at the [[Battle of Serroco]]. These missile launchers were located in the ship's bow and the lower protruding superstructure.<ref name="Kotor15" />

===Hangar complement===
The ''Kandosii''-type had a large cargo hold and could carry up to 128 starfighters for self-defense and offensive strikes. Usually the most common fighter was the [[Davaab-type starfighter|''Davaab''-type starfighter]]. Each dreadnaught could also carry eight [[Shaadlar-type troopship|''Shaadlar''-type troopships]] with 800 troops each. In addition to these, the ''Kandosii''-type could carry several [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]].<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==History==
[[File:Manddreadschem.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A Mandalorian Dreadnaught leading a fleet of troop ships to [[Jebble]].]]
Originally designed and built by the [[Fenelar]], the Mandalorians stole the ''Kandosii''-type design, during their conquest and destruction of the species. These dreadnaughts were later used by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] during the Mandalorian Wars against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="KOTORCG" />

A Mandalorian Dreadnaught led a [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] of [[Jehavey'ir-type assault ship|''Jehavey'ir''-type assault ships]] during the [[Battle of Serroco]], where the fleet bombarded the planet's surface with nuclear missiles.<ref name="Kotor15" /> Later in the war, a ship of this type lead by [[Cassus Fett]] [[Disaster on Jebble|bombarded the surface]] of [[Jebble]] to stop the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|Rakghoul plague]] from spreading to other systems.<ref name="Kotor28">[[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 28]]</ref>

===Legacy===
[[File:Vigiliance.jpg|180px|thumb|The ''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser, a Republic design based on the ''Kandosii''-type.]]
Sometime prior to [[100 BBY/Legends|100 BBY]], [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili Star Systems]] adopted a Mandalorian warship design to create the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]].<ref name="SWR:TDOR">''[[Star Wars: Republic: Dreadnaughts of Rendili]]''</ref> The design used in the Mandalorian Wars shared some superficial similarities with the Rendili Dreadnaught design. Additionally, some [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] designs like the [[Crusader-class corvette|''Crusader''-class corvette]], would also borrow the sharp-edged, aggressive look of the then-ancient warships.<ref name="DHB">{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=boards/viewtopic.php?p=155018|thread=KOTOR #15/16 art questions|user=[[Dustin Weaver]]|date=2007-05-09|time=14:07}}</ref>

==Known ships==
*''[[Gratua]]''
*''[[Parjai]]''

==Behind the scenes==
The reference to the ''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers being based on Mandalorian starships comes from ''The Dreadnaughts of Rendili''; combined with [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] statement later in the comic that the heavy cruisers would be turned into dungeon ships, it was clearly intended to show the development of the Mandalorian Dungeon Ship from ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''. However, [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']] article later made the dungeon ship specifically a separate ship class.

According to [[Dustin Weaver]], he was aware that the Mandalorian Dreadnaught was the basis of the ''Dreadnaught''-class Heavy Cruiser, but deliberately opted for a different appearance, citing changes over the millennia and a more aggressive look. He based the design on the [[Crusader-class corvette|''Crusader''-class corvette]] and the [[Lictor-class dungeon ship|''Lictor''-class dungeon ship]]. He also stated that they were produced by [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]], but this is not corroborated with the later Campaign Guide.<ref name="DHB" />
